summary: Tested new multi-provider route optimization feature (Circuit, Google Maps, Apple Maps). All 13 backend tests pass. Frontend UI working correctly with provider selection modal, optimization results, and provider badges on gig cards.
critical_code_review_comments: GoogleMapsService and AppleMapsService correctly implement local fallback when API keys are not configured
RoutesController properly handles provider selection via POST body {provider: 'google_maps'|'apple_maps'|'circuit'}
GET /api/routes/providers endpoint returns all 3 providers with correct configured status
OptimizationProvider field correctly stored in RoutePlan entity and returned in API responses
Frontend provider selection modal shows all 3 providers with API Ready/Local Fallback badges
Optimization results display stop order, distance, and duration estimates
seed_data_creation: Used existing gigs with orders for optimization testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Multi-provider route optimization feature fully implemented and tested. Circuit has real API key (shows 'API Ready'), Google Maps and Apple Maps use dummy keys (show 'Local Fallback'). All providers fall back to local optimization when API fails. Frontend shows provider selection modal with 3 cards, optimization results with stop order, and provider badges on gig cards.

evidence: Returns circuit, google_maps, apple_maps
Each provider has id, name, description, configured, icon fields
Circuit shows configured=true (real API key)
Google Maps and Apple Maps show configured=false (dummy keys)
Returns status=optimized, provider=google_maps_local
Local fallback works correctly when API key is dummy
Returns optimized stop order with recipient names and addresses
Returns status=optimized, provider=apple_maps_local
Local fallback works correctly when credentials are dummy
Returns optimized stop order with recipient names and addresses
Returns status=optimized, provider=circuit_local
Falls back to local when Circuit API fails (invalid key error)
Graceful fallback without error message
Empty body {} defaults to circuit provider
Returns circuit or circuit_local provider
Gig list includes optimizationProvider for optimized gigs
Field is nullable for older gigs (before migration)
Valid values: circuit, circuit_local, google_maps, google_maps_local, apple_maps, apple_maps_local
Gig detail includes optimizationProvider in plan object
Optimize button present on all gig cards with data-testid='optimize-gig-{id}'
Modal opens with 'Optimize Route' title
Modal shows 'Select a route optimization provider:' text
Circuit card with 'API Ready' badge (green)
Google Maps card with 'Local Fallback' badge
Apple Maps card with 'Local Fallback' badge
Each card has icon, name, description, and status badge
Clicking Google Maps card triggers POST /api/routes/{id}/optimize with provider=google_maps
Clicking Apple Maps card triggers POST /api/routes/{id}/optimize with provider=apple_maps
Loading spinner shows during optimization
Success message with provider badge (e.g., 'Google Maps (Local)')
Total Distance and Est. Duration displayed
Optimized Stop Order section with numbered stops
Each stop shows recipient name and address
Warning message about API not configured
Badge shows 'Optimized (Google Maps (Local))'
Badge shows 'Optimized (Apple Maps (Local))'
Badge shows 'Optimized (Circuit (Local))'
Provider name displayed in parentheses
Toast shows 'Route optimized with Google Maps (Local)!'
Toast shows 'Route optimized with Apple Maps (Local)!'
Toast appears in bottom-right corner

api_endpoints_tested: POST /api/routes/{id}/optimize without provider - PASS (defaults to circuit)
ui_flows_tested: Click Optimize button on gig card
View provider selection modal with 3 providers
Click Google Maps provider and view results
Click Apple Maps provider and view results
View provider badges on gig cards

mocked_apis.Google Maps Directions API: Uses dummy key - falls back to local optimization
mocked_apis.Apple Maps Server API: Uses dummy credentials - falls back to local optimization
mocked_apis.Circuit API: Has real key but API may fail - falls back to local optimization
